subject: English
chapter: The Summer of the Beautiful White Horse

formulas.name: Imagery
formulas.notes: Imagery helps to create a mental picture of the scene or character.
formulas.formula: The use of language to create vivid sensory experiences for the reader

formulas.name: Symbolism
formulas.notes: Symbolism adds depth and meaning to the story by representing complex ideas.
formulas.formula: The use of objects, colors, or other elements to represent abstract ideas or concepts

formulas.name: Alliteration
formulas.notes: Alliteration creates a musical or rhythmic effect in language.
formulas.formula: The repetition of initial consonant sounds in words that are close together

formulas.name: Personification
formulas.notes: Personification helps to create a more vivid and engaging description of the world around us.
formulas.formula: The attribution of human qualities or characteristics to non-human entities, such as objects or animals

formulas.name: Irony
formulas.notes: Irony can be used to highlight the absurdity or contradiction in a situation.
formulas.formula: A discrepancy or contrast between what is expected and what actually happens

formulas.name: Foreshadowing
formulas.notes: Foreshadowing creates suspense and anticipation in the reader.
formulas.formula: A hint or suggestion of events that will occur later in the story

formulas.name: Theme
formulas.notes: The theme is the central idea or message of the story.
formulas.formula: The underlying message or idea that the story conveys

formulas.name: Characterization
formulas.notes: Characterization helps to create a believable and engaging character.
formulas.formula: The process of creating a character's personality, traits, and motivations

formulas.name: Conflict
formulas.notes: Conflict creates tension and drive in the story.
formulas.formula: A problem or challenge that a character must overcome
